prompts: The circulatory system consists of the heart and blood vessels, facilitating blood flow throughout the body. The heart acts as a pump, ensuring blood circulation through two main pathways: pulmonary and systemic circulation. Oxygen-poor blood enters the right atrium, moves to the right ventricle, and is pumped to the lungs via pulmonary arteries. In the lungs, blood becomes oxygenated, returns to the left atrium, then enters the left ventricle. The left ventricle pumps oxygen-rich blood through the aorta to the entire body. The cycle continues as oxygen-depleted blood returns to the heart via veins, completing the circulation loop.
